heading: Exemption from tax of domestic goods purchased for the United States
label: 7510 Exemption from tax of domestic goods purchased for the United States
text: The privilege existing by provision of law on , or thereafter of purchasing supplies of goods imported from foreign countries for the use of the United States, duty free, shall be extended, under such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe, to all articles of domestic production which are subject to tax by the provisions of this title.December 1, 1873
notes: Amendments1976— struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 94–455
amendments: Amendments1976— struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 94–455
